q: Which statement best describes a homologous series of organic compounds?
o: A series of compounds that all have branched chains
A series of elements with similar physical properties
A series of compounds having the same general formula, similar chemical properties, and consecutive members differing by a –CH2– group
A series of compounds with different functional groups but the same molecular formula

q: Which of the following products is formed when carbon dioxide reacts with calcium hydroxide (lime water)?
What is the process called in which living organisms reproduce the beginning of a new generation?
The speed of sound (v) is calculated by multiplying which two wave properties?
The formula unit mass of a substance is the sum of the atomic masses of all atoms in a formula unit of a/an:
The distributions of electrons in the K, L, and M shell of the chlorine atom are:
What is the type of reproduction in which a new generation begins from the male gamete called?
Two wires, A and B, of the same material, have the same length, but the cross-sectional area of wire A is twice that of wire B. The ratio of their resistances RA : RB is:
A student connects a voltmeter across the terminals of a glowing bulb. What does the voltmeter measure?
A village is situated far from a river, but the groundwater level there is unpredictable and irregular. Which method should be adopted to provide water to the fields?
According to Newton's second law of motion, what happens when an unbalanced force is applied to an object?
o: The object moves in a circular path
The object's momentum changes in the direction of the applied force
The object comes to rest immediately

q: Where is the centre of curvature of a convex mirror located?
o: At the pole of the mirror
At the principal focus of the mirror

q: Which of the following correctly shows the unique nature of an endothermic reaction?
o: Endothermic reaction → Sodium hydroxide + hydrogen gas
Endothermic reaction → Sodium hydroxide → Sodium chloride + water

q:
Select the correct statement from the following.
A) The large spaces between gas particles allow them to be easily squeezed into smaller volumes.
B) Gas particles have a high average kinetic energy.
The expression for acceleration due to gravity on the surface of a planet of mass M and radius R is:
Which of the following is a molecule of a compound?
What happens if one drop of acid rain water is put on blue litmus paper?
o: Blue litmus paper will turn yellow
No colour changes in litmus paper
Blue litmus paper will turn black
Blue litmus paper will turn red

q: What type of epithelial tissue is the outer skin made of?
A particle has zero kinetic energy. What can be concluded about the particle's motion?
o: It is moving with high acceleration
It is moving with uniform velocity

q: Regarding the nucleus, which of the following statements is NOT correct?
o: The nuclear membrane is double-layered and porous.
The nuclear membrane plays an important role in the exchange of materials.

q: Which of the following statements is NOT true according to the sign convention for spherical lenses?
o: The focal length of a convex lens is positive.
The focal length of a concave lens is negative.
Object height is negative when the object is placed above the principal axis.

q: A particle's velocity-time graph is a straight line with a negative slope. What does this indicate about the particle's motion?
o: The particle is speeding up in the positive direction.
The particle is moving with increasing acceleration.
The particle is slowing down if moving in the positive direction.

q: What are the main components of a neuron?
o: Dendrites, cell body, axon and nerve endings
Dendrites, cell body and nerve endings
Cell body, dendrites, nerve-endings and synapse

q: By which of the following methods do some plants produce a new plant from a vegetative part (such as a root) of the parent plant?
Which of the following human activities contributes to ozone layer depletion?
o: Using CFC-based refrigerants and spray cans

q: The cost price of a box of notebooks is ₹7,400. The shopkeeper gave 28% discount on the cost price. What will be the selling price (in ₹)?
Which of the following is NOT a prime number?
The average age of 43 students of a class is 13 years. If the age of the teacher is also included, the average age of the whole group becomes 14 years. The age (in years) of the teacher is:
When x is added to each of 10, 16, 38 and 28, then the numbers so obtained, in this order, are in proportion. Then, if 3x : y :: y : (5x − 6), and y > 0, what is the value of y?

A class of 75 students took a Physics test. 22 students had an average score of 85. The other students had an average score of 95. What is the average score (rounded off to one decimal place) of the whole class?
A train of length 239 m takes 20 seconds to pass another train 161 m long coming from the opposite direction. If the speed of the first train is 43 km/hr, then find the speed of the second train.

Maya buys a mobile phone for her child. The phone is marked at ₹21,000 and is offered with two successive discounts of 5% and Z%, respectively. After receiving both discounts, she pays ₹11,172. Find the value of Z.
R's age is twice S's age and three times T's age. If the difference between the ages of S and T is 5 years, then what is the difference between the ages of R and T?
The number of prime numbers lying between 309 and 320 is:
The income of Raman is ₹43,800. He saves 20% of his income. If his income increases by 36% and expenditure increases by 40%, then his savings will:
If the radius of the base of a right circular cylinder is decreased by 15% and its height is increased by 149%, then what is the percentage increase (rounded to the nearest integer) in its volume?

Simplify the following.
(a^3 − b^3)/(a^2 + ab + b^2) + (a^3 + b^3)/(a^2 − ab + b^2)

In quadrilateral PQRS, the bisectors of ∠R and ∠S meet at the point T (inside the quadrilateral) and ∠STR = 42.5°. If the ratio of ∠P to ∠Q is 8 : 9, then what is the difference between the measures of ∠Q and ∠P?
From the top of a building 15 m high, the angle of depression to a point on the ground is 45 degrees. Find the horizontal distance from the building to the point.
